name: environment_near_miss_event_generates_redwood_car_fair
premise: A Environment field incident unit is in a fast-moving incident involving environment near miss event generates redwood car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases ecological harm, community impact, and compliance. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Mateo Haddad
agents.identity: I am Mateo Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Environment when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Talia Park
agents.identity: I am Talia Park, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environment.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Mateo Haddad must decide under care vs fairness. What does Mateo Haddad do?
